LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PARAGRAPHS 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 52 AND 53. THOSE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYMENT ARE IRRELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PARAGRAPHS 14 AND 15 AS IRRELEVANT. THE ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT IS HABITUALLY AN AGGRESSIVE/ANGRY DRIVER IS IRRELEVANT AND IMPROPER CHARACTER EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF PROPERLY ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT WAS DRIVING IN AN ANGRY/AGGRESSIVE MANNER IMMEDIATELY PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT AT PARAGRAPHS 20 AND 22. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PHRASE "LONGSTANDING, HABITUALLY AGGRESSIVE AND ANGRY DRIVING AND" IN PARAGRAPH 44. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO THE REMAINDER OF PARAGRAPH 44 AND DENIES AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN PARAGRAPH C OF THE PRAYER. THE COURT STRIKES PARAGRAPH 10(D) OF THE PRAYER [ATTORNEY'S FEES] WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE A STATUTORY, LEGAL, OR CONTRACTUAL BASIS FOR THE PRAYER FOR FEES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976
LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF MOVING PARTY. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)

DECLARATION OF JOSEPH P. BRENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MOTLEY, PATRICE RENEE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL GREG CHIN USED TO WORK FOR THE COURT, BUT THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT HIS PRIOR EMPLOYMENT IS A BASIS TO RECUSE ITSELF FROM THIS CASE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. CAUSE OF ACTION 1 SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A CLAIM FOR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT. SEE CARLSEN V. KOIVUMAKI (2014) 227 CAL.APP.4TH 879, 895. CAUSE OF ACTION 3 SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S AGGRESSIVE AND DANGEROUS DRIVING, CAUSING THE ACCIDENT, AND THEN FLEEING THE SCENE OF THE ACCIDENT DEMONSTRATE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT WITH RECKLESS DISREGARD TO CAUSING EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTRADICT THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT AND/OR ARE A SHAM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Set For Trial On Jul-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH P. BRENT OF BRENT, FIOL & PRATT LLP, TEL. NO. 984-0415 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEFANO G. FORMICA OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. (213)580-3952 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to paragraphs 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 52 and 53. Those allegations regarding defendant's employment are irrelevant to plaintiff's claim. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to paragraphs 14 and 15 as irrelevant. The allegation that defendant is habitually an aggressive/angry driver is irrelevant and improper character evidence. Plaintiff properly alleges that defendant was driving in an angry/aggressive manner immediately prior to the accident at paragraphs 20 and 22. The court grants the motion without leave to amend as to the phrase "longstanding, habitually aggressive and angry driving and" in paragraph 44. The court denies the motion as to the remainder of paragraph 44 and denies as to the prayer for punitive damages in paragraph c of the prayer. The court strikes paragraph 10(d) of the prayer [attorney's fees] without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite a statutory, legal, or contractual basis for the prayer for fees. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH P. BRENT OF BRENT, FIOL & PRATT LLP, TEL. NO. 984-0415 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEFANO G. FORMICA OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. (213)580-3952 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - The court discloses that defense counsel Greg Chin used to work for the court, but the court does not believe that his prior employment is a basis to recuse itself from this case. The matter was argued. DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Cause of action 1 sufficiently pleads a claim for willful misconduct. See Carlsen v. Koivumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 895. Cause of action 3 sufficiently pleads a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff's allegations regarding defendant's aggressive and dangerous driving, causing the accident, and then fleeing the scene of the accident demonstrate outrageous conduct with reckless disregard to causing emotional distress. The court rejects defendant's argument that the allegations in the amended complaint contradict the original complaint and/or are a sham. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Continued To Dec-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT NIRAV TOLIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Off calendar per request of moving party. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
